strengths: Strong backend and distributed-systems foundation from high-throughput production environments (Dunnhumby, RIDE Capital) with real-time API design and database scaling experience
Currently hands-on with agentic frameworks and modern LLM orchestration (MCP code generators, agentic workflows at re:cinq) showing recent technical currency
20+ years engineering experience with demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing culture (workshops, code dojos, team development)
concerns: Agentic AI production experience is only 2 months old (started April 2025 at re:cinq); insufficient depth for role requiring demonstrated multi-agent systems operating at scale with feedback loops, evaluation pipelines, and failure-mode mitigation

No evidence of team leadership at the scale required; resume shows individual-contributor and senior-engineer roles but lacks clear track record of growing engineering organizations from 10 to 30+ people with accountability models
Zero fintech infrastructure background despite role being explicitly fintech-focused; no experience with credit systems, payment flows, embedded finance, or regulated financial environments
response.justification: The candidate has solid backend systems engineering and current technical familiarity with agentic frameworks, but falls short on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, agentic AI experience spans only 2 months, insufficient to demonstrate production reliability, evaluation rigor, and failure-mode mitigation required for this role. No evidence of scaling engineering leadership (10→30+), and complete absence of fintech infrastructure or regulated-environment experience despite those being core to the position. The candidate would be stronger in a senior-IC or principal-engineer agentic-AI role where depth in a single domain is prioritized over executive breadth.
